_meta.notes: All credentials replaced with ${ENV_VAR} placeholders. Configure via Doppler or .env. Original had hardcoded Telegram bot token, Telegram channel chat_id, receiver secret, ngrok URL, Supabase URL, Telegram chat ID, public TG channel handle, AUTHOR identity strings — all replaced. Replace n8n credential IDs with your own after import.

name: YT Shorts Pilot v0.4 (full autonomy: GPT scenario -> render -> publish)

parameters.text: ={{ 'YT Pilot v0.4: no queued topics for ' + $json.today + '. Module A (G_A_topic_distiller) should have populated the queue — check.' }}

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  temperature: 0.7,
  max_tokens: 4000,
  response_format: {type: 'json_object'},
  messages: [
    {role: 'system', content: 'You are an AI architect generating scenario.json for a YouTube Short in SHORT_LAYOUT v2 spec. Tone: direct, concrete numbers, no hype. Return ONLY valid JSON.

Structure:
- 7 segments: 1=hook(5s), 2-6=body(6-7s), 7=cta(6s). Total ~45s.
- visual_type: hook_layered, numbered_macbook, project_chips, comparison_strikethrough, diagram_boxes, cta_buttons.
- At least 3 different visual_types across 7 segments.
- avatar_position: alternates top-right<->top-left, off for 2-3 segments.
- bottom_subtitle: curated 1-3 word UPPERCASE punch (not the voice_text). CTA = empty string.
- header_tag: > KEYWORD.kebab.

Each segment has: id, act, duration, start, voice_text, visual_type, visual_params, bottom_subtitle.

For numbered_macbook visual_params have sections[{number, title, subtitle, code_filename, code_lines:[{text,color}], cue_word, cue_color}].
For comparison_strikethrough — eyebrow, eyebrow_punch, strikethrough_items[], cue_word, cue_color, bottom_highlight.
For project_chips — title, subtitle, chips[], bottom_lead, bottom_punch.
For diagram_boxes — title, subtitle, boxes:[{text,color}], cue_word, cue_color.
For hook_layered — header_tag, shadow_text, punch_word, punch_color, small_caption, bottom_title.
For cta_buttons — lead, button_yes:{text,color}, button_no:{text,color}, username, cta.'},
    {role: 'user', content: 'Topic: ' + $json.title_ru + '
Slug: ' + $json.slug_en + '
Hype: ' + ($json.hype_score||'?') + '/100
Key points: ' + ($json.notes||'none')+'
Category: '+($json.topic_category||'?') + '

Generate scenario.json.'}
  ]
}) }}

parameters.text: ={{ 'YT Pilot v0.4 render fail slug=' + $('Build scenario payload').first().json.slug + ' error=' + ($json.error || 'unknown') + ' log=' + ($json.log || 'no log').toString().slice(-300) + '' }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ mp4_path: $json.mp4_path, title: $json.title, description: $json.description, tags: $json.tags, lang: $json.lang, privacy: $json.privacy }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ slug: $('Build YT payload').first().json.slug, video_id: $('POST /youtube/upload').first().json.video_id, youtube_url: $('POST /youtube/upload').first().json.url, tg_message_id: ($json.result && $json.result.message_id) || null, privacy: 'public', title: $('Build YT payload').first().json.title, notes: 'Auto-published by YT Shorts Pilot v0.4 (full autonomy: GPT scenario + render + upload)' }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'published', published_at: new Date().toISOString(), has_video: true, video_slug: $('Build YT payload').first().json.slug, notes: 'YT: ' + $('POST /youtube/upload').first().json.url }) }}

parameters.text: ={{ 'YT Pilot v0.4 ZERO-TOUCH DONE slug: ' + $('Build YT payload').first().json.slug + ' youtube: ' + $('POST /youtube/upload').first().json.url + ' hype: ' + $('Normalize topic').first().json.hype_score + ' render: ' + $('POST /render_queued (sync, up to 40 min)').first().json.duration_s + 's, upload: ' + $('POST /youtube/upload').first().json.duration_s + 's' }}
